title: Affine Filtering Measurements and Their Applications to Quantum Decoding
authors: Avijit Mandal, Noah Shutty, Henry D. Pfister, Stephen P. Jordan
abstract: Unambiguous state discrimination (USD) measurements are attractive because outcomes are either marked as conclusive (i.e., error free) or inconclusive (i.e., erased). We study affine filtering measurements, a structured variant of USD for decoding classical linear codes over pure-state classical-quantum channels, where a conclusive outcome identifies an affine subspace containing the transmitted codeword and an inconclusive outcome is treated as an erasure. For a group-covariant indexing of pure-state codewords, we show that the optimal design of affine filtering measurements is a semidefinite program that can be reduced to a linear program via character-based diagonalization. We use the resulting measurement to build a quantum decoding framework for local codes, and we demonstrate (via simulations on regular LDPC codes from Gallager ensembles using single parity check local constraints) that affine filtering based decoding can outperform symbol-wise USD and symbol-wise pretty good measurement based decoding methods on i.i.d. pure-state channels. In an independent and concurrent work, Buzet and Chailloux study similar fine-grained USD measurements for symmetric families of states. Their focus is on the code-agnostic setting whereas our focus is on code-aware constructions and decoding.
summary: This work introduces affine filtering measurements as an advanced tool for decoding classical linear codes transmitted via pure-state quantum channels. By mathematically reducing the optimal measurement design from a difficult Semidefinite Program to a solvable Linear Program using character theory, the authors provide a powerful new quantum decoding framework. Simulations confirm that this method significantly outperforms existing symbol-wise decoding techniques on structured codes like LDPC.
main_problem: Developing efficient quantum decoding methods for classical linear codes transmitted over pure-state classical-quantum (CQ) channels.
main_result: The proposed affine filtering measurement combined with Gaussian elimination outperforms symbol-wise USD and PGM decoders in simulations on i.i.d. pure-state channels for LDPC codes.
method: The optimal measurement design is formulated as a Semidefinite Program (SDP) which the authors successfully reduce to an equivalent Linear Program (LP) using character theory and symmetry arguments.
model_or_system: The system models quantum information processing where classical linear codes are encoded into pure-state quantum channels. The core mathematical tools involve group characters, SDP/LP optimization, and measurement operators (POVMs).
key_observables: Success probability ($P_{	ext{success}}$) of decoding; the expected number of recovered linear equations.
important_parameters: Code parameters like $(k, D)$ for LDPC codes; channel noise parameter $\alpha$; field size $q$.
main_assumptions: The derivation relies heavily on exploiting symmetries (coset symmetry) to reduce the SDP to an LP, and in some cases, passing to a limit ($\epsilon 	o 0$) by perturbing the state family.
figures_summary: Figures compare the success probability thresholds of the affine-filtering+GE decoder against qubit-wise USD/PGM decoders for various regular LDPC codes. Table 2 compares the expected number of linear equations recovered by different measurement types.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the problem (USD measurements) to developing the mathematical machinery: first, establishing the SDP formulation; second, using symmetry arguments and character theory to reduce this SDP to an equivalent LP; finally, applying this LP framework to simulate and compare decoding performance on specific LDPC codes.
why_interesting: The rigorous mathematical reduction of a complex quantum optimization problem (SDP) into a tractable linear program is a powerful technique applicable across quantum information theory, providing concrete tools for designing optimal measurements.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
